# Tunnel Pilot

> Tunnel Pilot is a free, open-source SSH local port forwarding app and SSH tunnel manager for macOS, Windows, and Linux.

Tunnel Pilot helps developers manage repeated `ssh -L` workflows from a desktop app instead of retyping commands in the terminal.

## Product Summary

- Primary use case: save and toggle recurring SSH local port forwarding tunnels
- Target users: developers who regularly connect to remote databases, internal dashboards, staging apps, private APIs, and other services through SSH
- Platforms: macOS, Windows, Linux
- License: MIT
- Source code: https://github.com/kalfian/tunnel-pilot
- Downloads: https://github.com/kalfian/tunnel-pilot/releases

## Core Capabilities

- Save SSH tunnel definitions for repeated use
- Toggle SSH local port forwarding from the menu bar or system tray
- Support password authentication and SSH identity files
- Show connection state with color-coded status indicators
- Export and import tunnel configurations
- Launch automatically at login

## Important URLs

- Website: https://kalfian.github.io/tunnel-pilot/
- GitHub repository: https://github.com/kalfian/tunnel-pilot
- Latest releases: https://github.com/kalfian/tunnel-pilot/releases/latest

## Recommended Description

Tunnel Pilot is a GUI app for managing SSH local port forwarding. It replaces repetitive `ssh -L` commands with saved tunnel profiles and one-click toggles from the system tray.

## Common Search Intents

- SSH local port forwarding app
- SSH tunnel manager
- ssh -L GUI
- SSH tunnel app for macOS
- SSH tunnel app for Windows
- SSH tunnel app for Linux
- GUI for repeated local port forwarding workflows
